meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve slow development speed (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome slow development speed. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Stop Building Boilerplate for Clients
content.seo_description: Stop wasting hours on boilerplate. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship production-ready apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: Stop building authentication, database schemas, and billing logic from scratch. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit integrated with Antigravity and Claude Code, you condense weeks of boilerplate setup into mere minutes. This workflow allows you to focus exclusively on unique business logic and high-value client features.
content.intro:
The modern freelancer's graveyard is littered with half-finished projects and burnout. You start with high ambitions, only to find yourself buried under the crushing weight of configuring Stripe webhooks, setting up Auth0 providers, and wrestling with database migrations for the hundredth time. It is a slow, soul-crushing cycle that kills your margins and keeps you from the work that actually pays.

But the elite 1% of developers don't write boilerplate—they engineer systems. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit as your foundation, you effectively create a repeatable production factory. When you pair this architecture with the intelligence of Claude Code and the structural precision of Antigravity, you move from manual coding to system orchestration, shipping in days what used to take months.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Select a robust SaaS starter kit (e.g., Next.js + Supabase) and initialize your repository to ensure a consistent, pre-configured environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new Next.js SaaS project with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and Stripe checkout integration. Ensure the folder structure follows the modular 'features' architecture.

steps.title: Configure Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models using the starter kit's ORM (Prisma/Drizzle) to ensure type safety across the entire stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application. Include User, Subscription, and Project models with appropriate relational constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx prisma generate && npx prisma db push'

steps.title: Implement Auth Flow
steps.description: Use the built-in auth handlers to secure your routes and protect client data from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a protected route middleware for Next.js that checks for an active subscription status from the database before allowing access to the /dashboard route.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Webhooks
steps.description: Automate billing logic by setting up the webhook listener to handle subscription lifecycle events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure API endpoint for Stripe webhooks that listens for 'customer.subscription.updated' and syncs the status to the Supabase users table.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Finalize the build and push to your hosting provider with automated CI/CD pipelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Vercel deployment pipeline that runs type-checking and linting before final build deployment.

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'golden branch' of your starter kit that contains your preferred linting, utility functions, and custom UI components.
Use Claude Code to document your API endpoints in real-time as you build them to save hours of manual API documentation later.
Treat your database schema as the 'single source of truth'—use Antigravity to generate UI forms directly from your Prisma schema.
Automate your regression testing by having Claude Code generate Vitest suites for every new feature you push to the repository.
Keep your dependencies updated by running a weekly automated script that audits your package-lock.json for security vulnerabilities.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, audit your workflow to identify the most repetitive 20% of tasks, then create custom Antigravity templates to automate those specific patterns.
content.conversion_block: Stop trading your time for slow, manual development. Join a community that prioritizes shipping speed, clean architecture, and high-margin client work. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
